footer.tag: A national laundromat franchise. Cleaner. Safer. Faster.
footer.copyright: © AU Hydro LLC dba WaveMAX Laundry. All rights reserved.

hero.sub: WaveMAX is the #1 laundromat franchise on Entrepreneur's 2026 Franchise 500. Semi-absentee from day one, modern Electrolux equipment, recession-proof demand. Build a durable cash-flowing business with national brand support behind you.

why.title: A laundromat franchise built for serious operators.
why.lede: WaveMAX is a leading laundromat franchise offering scalable, semi-absentee ownership with strong unit economics and national brand support. Disciplined site selection, smart sizing, premium equipment, and a service model that resonates with working-class communities — that's the playbook.
why.prideful: This is not a set-it-and-forget-it franchise. We're looking for owners who treat clean operations as a revenue lever — partners who want to outperform the legacy "zombiemats" through disciplined execution.
why.tile1Text: ~9 hours/week, 3 days a week, 3 hours per day. Most franchisees don't hire a manager initially — the model is designed to scale without a constant on-site presence.
why.tile2Text: Lean staffing, durable equipment, no inventory to manage. Predictable cost structure with strong unit economics that compound across multi-unit operators.
why.tile3Text: Laundry is a need, not a want. Working-class families rely on laundromats out of necessity — the demand is steady through every economic cycle.
why.tile4Text: High-extraction commercial machines that move customers through faster, run cleaner, and last longer than what walk-in competitors operate.
why.tile5Text: Operators with one cash-flowing store typically open their second within 18 months. Site-selection support and proven playbook scale linearly.
why.tile6Text: Marketing, training, technology, vendor relationships — the back office every owner-operator wants but most can't build alone.

sauce.title: Right site. Right size. Right service. Right systems.
sauce.lede: Our growth strategy is built around four disciplines that compound into reliable unit economics — and a model that's repeatable across markets.
sauce.rightSiteText: Demographic targeting, competition analysis, and real-world location strategy built for sustained demand.
sauce.rightSizeText: Each location is sized to fit the market — balancing footprint, machine count, and operational efficiency.
sauce.rightServiceText: Self-serve, wash-dry-fold drop-off, and commercial accounts — three revenue streams from one footprint.
sauce.rightSystemsText: Card-pay technology, real-time analytics, vendor-managed supplies, and a proven operations playbook.

sanit.title: Cleaner washes. Better customer confidence.
sanit.p1: WaveMAX locations are designed with advanced sanitation technology that elevates customer confidence and improves garment care. Our mission is to bring safer, cleaner, more efficient laundry to underserved areas — and that starts with the water itself, not just the cycle.
sanit.p2: Each WaveMAX location is built to be a trusted neighborhood hub — high-tech machines, consistent staffing, and elevated cleanliness combine to deliver a better experience for customers and stronger repeat traffic for owners.

invest.title: What it takes to own a WaveMAX®.
invest.lede: We're looking for committed operators with the liquidity to fund a single location and the appetite to grow into multi-unit ownership. Most franchisees finance a portion of the build-out and reach positive cash flow within the first year of operation.
invest.card1Text: Cash on hand to qualify. Covers franchise fee + working capital + your share of build-out.
invest.card2Text: Range covers all-in build-out: equipment, build, working capital, opening marketing.
invest.card3Text: A representative store's net income — even with full financing in place. Item 19 has full details.

faq.title: WaveMAX® franchise FAQ
faq.lede: The questions we hear most often during discovery. The full FAQ lives on /faq/ — these are the headlines.
faq.a1: It's a semi-absentee business from day one. We recommend being in the store about 9 hours per week — 3 days a week, 3 hours per day. Most franchisees don't hire a dedicated manager initially; the model is designed to scale without one.
faq.a2: Blue-collar, working-class families who rely on laundromats out of necessity, not luxury. The customer is steady through every economic cycle. We also serve Airbnb hosts, restaurants, gyms, and medical offices on the commercial side.
faq.a3: Total investment runs $1M–$1.5M; we look for $400K of liquidity to qualify. That covers franchise fee, build-out, equipment, working capital, and opening marketing. Item 19 of the FDD has the detailed breakdown.
faq.q4: Will WaveMAX help me find a site?
faq.a4: Yes. We guide you through working-class ZIP codes, demographic targeting, and brand-approved locations before lease sign-off. Site selection is the single highest-leverage decision in this business and we don't leave it to chance.
faq.q5: How big is WaveMAX right now?
faq.a5: 75+ stores and growing, supported by quality unit economics, franchisee satisfaction, and a scalable national playbook. Ranked #1 laundromat franchise on Entrepreneur's 2026 Franchise 500. We build durable, cash-flowing businesses — not just franchises.
faq.a6: Multi-unit ownership is the path most franchisees take. Operators with one cash-flowing store typically open their second within 18 months. Territory rights and area-development incentives are available for serious multi-unit operators.

cta.title: Start the conversation.
cta.lede: Tell us about yourself and we'll send the FDD and walk you through the process. No pressure — most owners take 3–6 months from first call to lease signed.

numbers.title: Real numbers. Verified FDD.
numbers.lede: Item 19 of the 2025 FDD reports a 50-store sample of WaveMAX locations. Every prospective franchisee receives these numbers during discovery — they're legally regulated, and we publish them up front.
numbers.jaxFlowText: Operating cash flow before debt service. Royalty fee, marketing fund contribution, and POS/technology fees already deducted.
numbers.jaxIncomeText: Net income after the equipment financing payment is also deducted — modeled with 100% SBA-eligible financing, the most leveraged scenario.
numbers.attr: Source: WaveMAX Franchise Disclosure Document, 2025 issue, Item 19. Past performance of one company-owned unit is not a guarantee of future franchisee results.

loc.demoText: Working-class ZIP codes with renter density, household income bands, and stable population growth.
loc.sizeText: Footprint matched to market: machine count, dryer ratio, queue capacity tuned for projected volume.
loc.trafficText: Drive-by counts, anchor co-tenancy, and pedestrian access modeled — visibility is half the marketing.

equipment.title: Modern Electrolux. Programmed for performance.
equipment.lede: Soft-mount, high-G extract, programmable controllers, online diagnostics, and corporate-negotiated parts and service. The result: faster cycles, lower utility cost per pound, and predictable maintenance.
equipment.partner1: Preferred pricing on the full equipment package
equipment.partner3: Nationwide service network and parts supply
equipment.partner5: AWS-only washers; only dryer with Eco Power
equipment.sanit4: Brighter whites, softer fabric, no odor
equipment.disclaimer: Premium equipment claims (450G extract, 80lb large-format, UV sanitization) only appear on store pages where the equipment is verified as installed.
equipment.tourTitle: Walk through a real WaveMAX, room by room
equipment.tourLede: Corporate's published 3D Matterport walkthrough — explore the floor plan, equipment layout, and customer experience in your browser. Or visit our curated photo gallery on /virtual-tour/.

process.title: Six steps from first call to grand opening.
process.lede: Typical timeline is 6–9 months. The pace is mostly site-availability- and buildout-driven; if a candidate already has a site identified and pre-approved, the timeline compresses.
process.step1Text: 30-minute exploratory conversation. Market, capital, timeline, fit.
process.step2Text: Receive and sign the Franchise Disclosure Document. Item 19 detail.
process.step3Text: Demographic + traffic study. Territory selection. Lender intros.
process.step4Text: Visit corporate HQ in Jacksonville. Meet the team and tour stores.
process.step5Text: Execute the agreement. Site approval. Lease negotiation.
process.step6Text: Multi-week training. Buildout. Opening marketing. Grand opening.

reviews.title: What customers say across the network
reviews.lede: Real 5-star Google reviews, sampled live from operating WaveMAX franchise locations. Every card links to the store that earned the review.
reviews.loading: Loading reviews from across the network…

hero.sub: WaveMAX Laundry is a national franchise network on a mission to deliver the gold standard in laundry service. Founded in 2012 by Mike Roberts, headquartered in Jacksonville, Florida, and operated under AU Hydro LLC dba WaveMAX Laundry.

story.title: Built to modernize an industry stuck in the past.
story.p1: WaveMAX was founded in 2012 by Mike Roberts in Jacksonville, Florida. After two decades building successful businesses across multiple industries, Mike became fascinated with the laundromat industry's stability and cash flow potential — and equally struck by how outdated the average laundromat had become as a customer experience.
story.p2: The thesis behind WaveMAX: laundry is a steady, recession-resistant business, but most laundromats run as broken legacy operations — neglected equipment, poor lighting, no staffing, no systems. Modernize the design, professionalize the operations, and franchise the playbook, and the result is a category-defining experience that customers actually choose, not just settle for.
story.p3: Today the system operates as AU Hydro LLC dba WaveMAX Laundry, with 75+ open locations and a growing network of independent franchise owners across the United States.

quote.text: I founded WaveMAX with a simple mission: to help entrepreneurs build profitable, sustainable laundromat businesses that deliver superior returns while enhancing their lifestyle rather than consuming it.

awards.title: Independently verified.
awards.lede: WaveMAX is one of the few laundromat franchises ranked on Entrepreneur's annual Franchise 500 — the industry's most rigorous independent benchmark of franchise system quality, growth, financial strength, and stability.
awards.a1Bio: Ranked #1 in the laundromat category on Entrepreneur's 2026 Franchise 500 list.
awards.a2Bio: Ranked #485 overall across all franchise systems and industries on Entrepreneur's 2026 Franchise 500.
awards.a3Bio: Ranked #416 overall on Entrepreneur's 2024 Franchise 500 — moved up to #485 in 2026 alongside the system's expansion.

team.title: The team behind the brand.
team.lede: WaveMAX is led by a small team of operators who have been running and scaling the business since 2012. Brand integrity, franchisee outcomes, and operational discipline are the standards every decision is measured against.
team.mrBio: Mike founded WaveMAX in 2012 after two decades building successful businesses across multiple industries. He leads brand strategy, franchisee development, and the long-term vision for the system.
team.shBio: Sheila has been a Managing Partner since the company's founding in 2012. Previously the President of Learning Time Academy, she leads finance, accounting, and capital planning for the franchise system.
team.bhBio: Brittany leads candidate intake and the franchise discovery process — from the first conversation through FDD review, Discovery Day, and award. The front door for prospective owners considering WaveMAX.

values.title: A national network. One operating standard.
values.lede: Every WaveMAX is independently owned and operated, but the system runs on a shared set of operating standards. These values shape both the customer experience inside the store and the long-term partnership we build with each owner.
values.v1Text: Modern equipment, attended floors, advanced sanitation in equipped stores. The customer should never have to wonder whether the machine is clean enough to use.
values.v2Text: Bright stores, attended hours, and disciplined site selection that puts WaveMAX locations in neighborhoods that read as safe to families running errands at night.
values.v3Text: High-extraction commercial machines move loads through faster than home machines or typical laundromats. Less waiting, more done.
values.v4Text: We are intentionally selective about who we award territories to. Every owner reflects the WaveMAX standard in their market.
values.v5Text: The success metric for any decision is whether it builds franchisee cash flow over the long term — not short-term royalty extraction.
values.v6Text: Site selection, equipment, training, marketing, and operations all run on documented playbooks. Repeatable across markets, not bespoke.

cta.title: Two ways to take the next step.
cta.lede: If you're a prospective franchisee, start with the franchise overview. If you're a customer, journalist, or partner with a different question, the contact page has the right channel.

hero.sub: WaveMAX is a network of 100+ independent franchise owners across the United States. Hear from a handful of them — Area Reps and multi-unit operators sharing what WaveMAX ownership actually looks like inside the system.

cards.title: Hear from WaveMAX owners directly.
cards.lede: Each of the franchisees below is a real WaveMAX owner with an active role in the network. Quotes are theirs, attributed by name and territory.
cards.slQuote: WaveMAX gives individuals the ability to operate a business, but also focus on other aspects of life. Operating within the WaveMAX framework, while still putting your own touch on your dream of owning a semi-absentee business, is the perfect formula. WaveMAX has figured out how to sophisticate an archaic industry through technology, marketing, area studies, and continued operational improvements to stay ahead of the competition.
cards.slRole: Area Rep & Store Owner · Gaithersburg, MD
cards.caQuote: I chose to get involved with WaveMAX because I saw a unique opportunity to bring something the laundromat industry desperately needed: professional management, operational structure, and a higher standard of excellence. Over the last seven to eight years, I've seen this vision come to life. What excites me most is where we're going — scaling with intention and building it together.
cards.caRole: Area Rep & Operator · DFW / East TX
cards.pkQuote: WaveMAX has truly transformed the customer experience by creating a safe, reliable, and welcoming environment that people trust and return to. The laundromat industry has historically been dominated by independent, coin-operated operations — poorly maintained, inconsistently staffed, and lacking any brand identity. WaveMAX was purpose-built as a scalable franchise system to fix that.
cards.shQuote: As the first WaveMAX franchisee, I was there when the brand was figuring out what it wanted to be. The discipline this team has brought to site selection, equipment, and operations is what separates WaveMAX from every other laundromat I've seen. I literally wrote the book on it — SHIFT Happens.
cards.shRole: Director of Operations · First WaveMAX Franchisee · Area Rep N. California / Nevada · Author of SHIFT Happens

callout.title: Our numbers, not just our quotes.
callout.lede: Owner stories are part of the picture, but they're not the whole picture. WaveMAX publishes verifiable Item 19 figures in our Franchise Disclosure Document, and each store's customer experience is reflected in live Google reviews — not cherry-picked excerpts.
callout.item1: 2024 FDD Item 19: $471,201 average gross revenue, $436,114 median, $713,934 top performer — sample of 50 operating stores
callout.item2: Jacksonville flagship: $217,762 net cash flow / $113,074 net income (with full equipment financing)
callout.item3: Reported EBITDA range: 25–35% across the system
callout.item4: Live customer reviews: every WaveMAX location's reviews are surfaced live from Google Places — the experience your customers will actually have
callout.item5: Brand recognition: ranked #1 laundromat franchise on Entrepreneur's 2026 Franchise 500, #485 overall in 2026 (up from #416 in 2024)

hero.sub: WaveMAX is the #1 laundromat franchise on Entrepreneur's 2026 Franchise 500. Below: the Item 19 figures, cash-flow examples, and the structural reasons operators choose this category. No cherry-picked numbers — verified, source-cited.

i19.title: 2024 average gross revenue per store.
i19.lede: Per the 2025 FDD Item 19 disclosure: a 50-store sample of WaveMAX locations reporting full-year 2024 gross revenue. These figures are the same numbers every prospective franchisee receives during the FDD review phase of the discovery process — they're legally regulated, and we publish them up front.
i19.attr: Source: WaveMAX Franchise Disclosure Document, 2025 issue, Item 19. Full document provided to qualified candidates during discovery (step 02).

flow.title: Jacksonville flagship — 2024 actuals.
flow.lede: The clearest read of the WaveMAX model is our company-owned Jacksonville flagship. Same FDD, same playbook, same cost structure as a franchisee unit — with the books open. 2024 actual results:
flow.cashLabel: Net cash flow (after royalties + marketing + POS)
flow.cashText: Operating cash flow before debt service. Royalty fee, marketing fund contribution, and POS / technology fees already deducted.
flow.incomeText: Net income after the equipment financing payment is also deducted — modeled as if the franchisee financed 100% of the equipment package via SBA-eligible financing, the most leveraged scenario.
flow.disclaimer: Past performance of one company-owned unit is not a guarantee of future franchisee results. See the FDD for full disclosures, Item 19 detail, and substantiation of all figures.

cat.title: Six structural reasons operators choose laundry.
cat.lede: Beyond the numbers, the laundromat category has structural characteristics that make it a durable cash-flow business — particularly when modernized, professionalized, and run as a franchise system rather than a one-off independent operation.
cat.r1Text: Laundry is a need, not a want. Working-class families rely on laundromats out of necessity — usage patterns are stable through every economic cycle.
cat.r2Text: The "product" is a service, not goods. No spoilage, no obsolescence, no inventory writedowns. Equipment depreciates predictably and is well-understood by lenders.
cat.r3Text: No accounts receivable. No collections. No 30-day payment terms. The model converts revenue to cash on the same day it's earned.
cat.r4Text: Modern attended-but-not-coin-operated stores run with a small team. Labor cost is a smaller percentage of revenue than retail or food service.
cat.r5Text: Equipment + build-out are SBA-eligible. Lenders who understand laundry economics structure financing accordingly. Most franchisees use a mix of cash + SBA debt.
cat.r6Text: The model is built for scale. Operators with one cash-flowing store typically open their second within 18 months, supported by area-development incentives.

ent.title: Independently ranked. Repeatedly recognized.
ent.lede: Entrepreneur's annual Franchise 500 is the franchise industry's most rigorous benchmark of system quality, growth, financial strength, and stability. WaveMAX has been ranked on it consistently — and has held the #1 spot in the laundromat category in 2026.

cta.title: Request the full investment guide.
cta.lede: The investment guide includes the full sources-and-uses, sample pro forma, financing structure, and the 6-step discovery roadmap. Delivered by email, no high-pressure sales call required.
